subject: general-knowledge
description: Arab Spring MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: The Arab Spring movement started in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Arab Spring began in Tunisia in December 2010 after the self-immolation of Mohamed Bouazizi, before spreading across the Middle East and North Africa.

mcqs.q: The Arab Spring began in which year?
mcqs.explanation: The Arab Spring began in December 2010 in Tunisia, though most uprisings in other countries occurred in 2011.

mcqs.q: The event that sparked the Arab Spring was?
options: Self-immolation of Mohamed Bouazizi in Tunisia
mcqs.explanation: Mohamed Bouazizi, a Tunisian street vendor, set himself on fire on 17 December 2010 in Sidi Bouzid, protesting police harassment and government corruption.

mcqs.q: Which country experienced a prolonged civil war leading to major humanitarian crises?
mcqs.explanation: Syria's Arab Spring uprising evolved into a devastating civil war (2011-2024) with over 500,000 deaths and millions of refugees.

mcqs.q: Who was the President of Syria during the Arab Spring uprisings?
mcqs.explanation: Bashar al-Assad was President of Syria when Arab Spring protests began in 2011. He remained in power until December 2024.

mcqs.q: Muammar Gaddafi belonged to which country?
mcqs.explanation: Muammar Gaddafi ruled Libya for 42 years (1969-2011) before being overthrown and killed during the Arab Spring.

mcqs.q: The Yemen Crisis started in?
mcqs.explanation: The Yemen Crisis began in 2011 with Arab Spring protests against President Ali Abdullah Saleh, escalating into civil war by 2014-2015.

mcqs.q: Mohamed Bouazizi was a?
mcqs.explanation: Mohamed Bouazizi was a 26-year-old Tunisian street vendor in Sidi Bouzid. His self-immolation ignited the Arab Spring.

mcqs.q: Mohamed Bouazizi died on?
mcqs.explanation: Mohamed Bouazizi set himself on fire on 17 December 2010 and died from his injuries on 4 January 2011.

mcqs.q: Tunisia's revolution was popularly called?
mcqs.explanation: Tunisia's 2010-2011 revolution was called the Jasmine Revolution, named after Tunisia's national flower.

mcqs.q: Who was the Tunisian President overthrown in January 2011?
mcqs.explanation: President Zine El Abidine Ben Ali, who had ruled Tunisia for 23 years (1987-2011), fled to Saudi Arabia on 14 January 2011.

mcqs.q: Ben Ali fled Tunisia to which country?
mcqs.explanation: Ben Ali fled to Saudi Arabia on 14 January 2011 where he lived in exile until his death in 2019.

mcqs.q: Hosni Mubarak ruled Egypt for how many years before being overthrown?
mcqs.explanation: Hosni Mubarak ruled Egypt for 30 years (1981-2011), ending with his resignation on 11 February 2011 after 18 days of Tahrir Square protests.

mcqs.q: Egyptian Revolution protests were centered at?
mcqs.explanation: Tahrir Square in Cairo was the epicenter of Egypt's 2011 revolution, where millions of protesters gathered for 18 days.

mcqs.q: Hosni Mubarak resigned on?
mcqs.explanation: Hosni Mubarak resigned on 11 February 2011 after 18 days of massive protests at Tahrir Square.

mcqs.q: Egypt's first democratically elected president was?
mcqs.explanation: Mohamed Morsi of the Muslim Brotherhood became Egypt's first democratically elected president in June 2012, ousted by a coup in July 2013.

mcqs.q: Which party did Mohamed Morsi belong to?
mcqs.explanation: Mohamed Morsi represented the Freedom and Justice Party — the political wing of the Muslim Brotherhood — in Egypt.

mcqs.q: Who led the military coup against Morsi in July 2013?
mcqs.explanation: Then-Defense Minister General Abdel Fattah el-Sisi led the military coup against President Mohamed Morsi on 3 July 2013.

mcqs.q: Muammar Gaddafi ruled Libya for how many years?
mcqs.explanation: Muammar Gaddafi ruled Libya for 42 years, from the 1969 coup that overthrew King Idris until his death on 20 October 2011.

mcqs.q: Gaddafi was killed in which city?
mcqs.explanation: Muammar Gaddafi was captured and killed by rebel forces on 20 October 2011 in his hometown of Sirte, Libya.

mcqs.q: Gaddafi was killed on?
mcqs.explanation: Muammar Gaddafi was killed on 20 October 2011 in Sirte, ending his 42-year rule of Libya.

mcqs.q: NATO intervened in Libya under which UN Security Council resolution?
mcqs.explanation: UN Security Council Resolution 1973 (17 March 2011) authorized a no-fly zone and military action to protect civilians in Libya.

mcqs.q: Syria's uprising began in which city?
mcqs.explanation: Syria's uprising began in Daraa in mid-March 2011 after the arrest and torture of schoolchildren who spray-painted anti-government graffiti.

mcqs.q: Bashar al-Assad belongs to which religious sect?
mcqs.explanation: Bashar al-Assad belongs to the Alawite sect, a minority Shia offshoot (~12% of Syrians), ruling over a Sunni-majority population.

mcqs.q: The Syrian Civil War caused approximately how many deaths?
mcqs.explanation: The Syrian Civil War caused over 500,000 deaths and displaced over 13 million people, with many fleeing as refugees to Turkey, Jordan, Lebanon, and Europe.

mcqs.q: The Yemeni President overthrown during Arab Spring was?
mcqs.explanation: Ali Abdullah Saleh, who had ruled Yemen for 33 years, stepped down in February 2012 after Arab Spring protests and an assassination attempt.

mcqs.q: Ali Abdullah Saleh stepped down in?
mcqs.explanation: Ali Abdullah Saleh formally stepped down on 27 February 2012, handing power to his deputy Abdrabbuh Mansur Hadi.

mcqs.q: Which rebel group captured Yemen's capital Sanaa in 2014?
mcqs.explanation: Houthi rebels (Ansar Allah) captured Yemen's capital Sanaa in September 2014, triggering the current phase of the civil war.

mcqs.q: Saudi Arabia-led coalition began military operations in Yemen in?
mcqs.explanation: The Saudi-led coalition, Operation Decisive Storm, began airstrikes in Yemen on 26 March 2015 against Houthi rebels.

mcqs.q: The Arab Spring protest site in Bahrain was?
mcqs.explanation: Pearl Roundabout in Manama became the epicenter of Bahrain's Arab Spring protests in February 2011. It was later demolished by the government.

mcqs.q: Which GCC-led force intervened in Bahrain in March 2011?
mcqs.explanation: The GCC's Peninsula Shield Force, led by Saudi Arabia and UAE, entered Bahrain on 14 March 2011 to suppress pro-democracy protests.

mcqs.q: Which country was considered the only successful democratic transition of the Arab Spring?
mcqs.explanation: Tunisia was widely considered the only Arab Spring country to achieve a successful democratic transition, with peaceful elections and a 2014 constitution. Its democracy has since eroded under President Kais Saied.

mcqs.q: Which social media platforms played a major role in the Arab Spring?
mcqs.explanation: Facebook and Twitter were heavily used by Arab Spring activists to organize protests, share videos, and bypass state-controlled media, giving rise to the term 'Facebook Revolution'.

mcqs.q: Which news network extensively covered the Arab Spring uprisings?
mcqs.explanation: Al Jazeera, based in Qatar, provided extensive coverage of Arab Spring uprisings, playing a significant role in spreading information across the Arab world.

mcqs.q: The Muslim Brotherhood was founded in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Muslim Brotherhood (Ikhwan al-Muslimeen) was founded in Egypt by Hassan al-Banna in 1928. It became a major political force during the Arab Spring.

mcqs.q: Abdel Fattah el-Sisi became Egypt's President in?
mcqs.explanation: Abdel Fattah el-Sisi won Egypt's presidential election in May 2014 and took office on 8 June 2014, a year after the coup that ousted Morsi.

mcqs.q: Libya's capital that saw major fighting during Arab Spring was?
mcqs.explanation: Tripoli, Libya's capital, saw major fighting in August-October 2011 as rebel forces advanced and captured Gaddafi's compound at Bab al-Azizia.

mcqs.q: Benghazi became famous in 2011 as the?
mcqs.explanation: Benghazi, Libya's second-largest city, became the stronghold of anti-Gaddafi rebels from February 2011, and later the site of the 2012 US consulate attack.

mcqs.q: The Arab Spring sparked protests in how many Arab countries?
mcqs.explanation: The Arab Spring sparked protests in over 15 Arab countries including Tunisia, Egypt, Libya, Yemen, Syria, Bahrain, Algeria, Jordan, Morocco, Iraq, Kuwait, Lebanon, Mauritania, Oman, and Sudan.

mcqs.q: The Russian-backed faction in Syria's civil war was?
mcqs.explanation: Russia backed Bashar al-Assad's government in Syria's civil war, intervening militarily from September 2015 onwards.

mcqs.q: Which terrorist group emerged from the chaos of the Syrian Civil War?
mcqs.explanation: ISIS (Islamic State in Iraq and Syria), proclaimed the caliphate in 2014, emerging from the chaos of the Syrian Civil War and post-US Iraq.

mcqs.q: The Libyan National Transitional Council was formed in?
mcqs.explanation: The National Transitional Council (NTC) was formed in February 2011 by opposition leaders during the Libyan Civil War.

mcqs.q: Who became the face of Egypt's April 6 Youth Movement?
mcqs.explanation: Ahmed Maher was co-founder of the April 6 Youth Movement, a major pro-democracy group that organized Egypt's 2011 protests.

mcqs.q: Wael Ghonim was associated with Arab Spring because he?
options: Created 'We are all Khaled Said' Facebook page
mcqs.explanation: Wael Ghonim, a Google executive, anonymously created the 'We are all Khaled Said' Facebook page that mobilized Egyptian protesters in 2011.

mcqs.q: The Arab Spring resulted in the rise of refugees from which country the most?
mcqs.explanation: Syria produced the largest Arab Spring refugee crisis, with over 6 million Syrian refugees and 7 million internally displaced by 2024.

mcqs.q: Which Arab Spring country became a failed state?
mcqs.explanation: Libya descended into a failed state after Gaddafi's fall, with rival governments, militias, and two civil wars (2011, 2014-2020).

mcqs.q: Syria's main opposition coalition was called?
mcqs.explanation: The Syrian National Coalition (SNC) formed in November 2012 as the main umbrella opposition group against Bashar al-Assad.

mcqs.q: The Free Syrian Army was established in?
mcqs.explanation: The Free Syrian Army (FSA) was established on 29 July 2011 by defected Syrian Armed Forces officers to fight against Assad's forces.

mcqs.q: Bashar al-Assad was finally overthrown in?
mcqs.explanation: Bashar al-Assad was overthrown in December 2024 after a rapid offensive by Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS) rebels captured Damascus. Assad fled to Russia.

mcqs.q: Who replaced Assad as Syria's interim leader in December 2024?
mcqs.explanation: Ahmed al-Sharaa (nom de guerre Abu Mohammed al-Julani), leader of Hayat Tahrir al-Sham, became Syria's interim leader after Assad's fall in December 2024.

mcqs.q: The Algerian protests during Arab Spring primarily demanded?
mcqs.explanation: Algerian Arab Spring protests (2010-2012) demanded political reform, economic improvement, and ending the state of emergency, but did not overthrow the government.

mcqs.q: Jordan's response to Arab Spring was?
mcqs.explanation: Jordan's King Abdullah II responded with constitutional reforms, government reshuffles, and economic measures to prevent major upheaval.

mcqs.q: Morocco's response to Arab Spring was?
mcqs.explanation: Morocco held a constitutional referendum on 1 July 2011 under King Mohammed VI, granting more powers to parliament while maintaining the monarchy.

mcqs.q: The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) response to Arab Spring was?
mcqs.explanation: The GCC (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, Oman) broadly supported the status quo, intervening militarily in Bahrain and economically in Egypt and Jordan.

mcqs.q: Qatar's role in Arab Spring was notable for?
mcqs.explanation: Qatar was one of the few Gulf states that actively supported some Arab Spring uprisings, particularly in Libya and Egypt, largely through Al Jazeera coverage.

mcqs.q: Sudan's Arab Spring-era protests eventually led to the fall of?
mcqs.explanation: Sudan's Omar al-Bashir, who ruled for 30 years, was overthrown on 11 April 2019 after months of protests — often called a delayed Arab Spring wave.

mcqs.q: The Algeria protests that led to Bouteflika's resignation in 2019 were?
mcqs.explanation: Algeria's Hirak Movement began in February 2019 and led to President Abdelaziz Bouteflika's resignation in April 2019 after 20 years in power.

mcqs.q: Which Arab Spring country had a successful power-sharing government after its uprising?
mcqs.explanation: Tunisia formed a power-sharing government between the Islamist Ennahda party and secular parties after its 2011 revolution — the only such successful outcome.

mcqs.q: Which Egyptian opposition figure won the 2005 Nobel Peace Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Mohamed ElBaradei, former IAEA Director General, won the 2005 Nobel Peace Prize and became a prominent Egyptian opposition figure during the Arab Spring.

mcqs.q: Which Arab Spring phrase became internationally famous?
options: The people want the fall of the regime
mcqs.explanation: 'The people want the fall of the regime' (Ash-sha'b yurid isqat an-nizam) was the iconic slogan chanted across Arab Spring protests in multiple countries.

mcqs.q: The 2019 Sudanese revolution ended which dictator's rule?
mcqs.explanation: Omar al-Bashir's 30-year rule of Sudan (1989-2019) ended on 11 April 2019 following mass protests, often seen as part of the Arab Spring's second wave.
